metadata.description: Route database schema (dual-country joins) and tech stack recommendation for pet transport site
metadata.dependencies: Block 5 (country regs), Block 6 (country regs), Block 7 (airline DB, breed restrictions)

route_database_schema.overview: Each route page draws from TWO country records (origin export + destination import), multiple airline records, and breed restriction records. The schema supports these dual-country joins. Route data is assembled by the Librarian from separate source tables and exported as a single JSON file per page for the Builder to consume.
design_principles: One record per page. Slug is the primary key. Updates overwrite, never duplicate.
Dual-country join: origin.export_requirements + destination.import_requirements = route regulatory data.
All data ingested from source workers (Geographer, Wordsmith, Interrogator, Optimiser, Connector) is validated against schema before storage.
Every record has version number and updated_at timestamp.
Content status pipeline: draft -> seo_optimised -> humanised -> audited -> published.

pages.description: Central table. One record per page on the site.

template.description: Which template the Builder uses to render this page.

status.description: Content pipeline status. Controls whether Builder can generate the page.

origin_code.description: ISO alpha-2 country code. Required for route-page and origin-hub templates.

destination_code.description: ISO alpha-2 country code. Required for route-page and country-guide templates.

content.description: Full content object from Wordsmith/Chameleon. Contains h1, overview, sections array, word_count.

faqs.description: FAQ array from Interrogator. Each item has question and answer.

seo.description: SEO metadata from Optimiser. Contains title, meta_description, canonical, og_image, schema markup.

links.description: Link graph from Connector. Contains upward, cross_type, and sideways link arrays.

route_data.description: Assembled route data. Librarian joins origin export + destination import + airlines + breeds into this object.

version.description: Version number. Increments on every update.

import_requirements.description: Full import requirements from Geographer. Copied from countries_pet_regulations.json.

classification_by_others.description: How other countries classify this country (CDC risk, AU group, SG category, etc.)

last_verified.description: When regulations were last verified against official sources.

countries.initial_data: 10 P1 countries from countries_pet_regulations.json (UK, USA, UAE, Australia, Canada, Germany, France, Singapore, Hong Kong, South Africa)

airlines.description: Airline pet transport policies. Source: Geographer (airline_pet_policies.json).

cabin_allowed.description: Does airline allow pets in cabin?

cargo_allowed.description: Does airline transport pets as cargo?

cargo_suspended.description: Is cargo pet service currently suspended?

brachycephalic_policy.description: How airline handles brachycephalic breeds in cargo.

routes_p1.description: Key P1 routes this airline serves.

banned_countries.description: Array of country codes where this breed is banned from import.

restricted_countries.description: Array of country codes where this breed requires special permit.

airline_cargo_banned.description: Airlines that ban this breed from cargo.

airline_cargo_restricted.description: Airlines that restrict (seasonal/conditional) this breed in cargo.

content_status_log.description: Audit trail for content pipeline status changes.

route_data_assembly.description: How the Librarian assembles route data for a single route page.
steps: 1. Pull countries['AE'].export_requirements (UAE export: municipality export permit, MOCCAE endorsement, vet inspection within 48hrs)
2. Pull countries['GB'].import_requirements (UK import: microchip, rabies, 21-day wait, AHC, tapeworm for dogs, approved route, breed check)
3. Pull countries['GB'].banned_breeds and match against breeds table (Pit Bull, Tosa, Dogo Argentino, Fila Brasileiro)
4. Query airlines where routes_p1 includes both AE and GB (Emirates SkyCargo, BA cargo, Etihad cargo, potentially Lufthansa/KLM connecting)
5. Pull classification_by_others: how UK classifies UAE (listed country), how USA_CDC classifies UAE (high-risk, for onward context)
6. Calculate route_complexity based on destination requirements (quarantine? titre test? breed bans? timeline?)
8. Merge with content, faqs, seo, links from pages table
9. Export as single JSON file for Builder

timeline_steps.timing: As required by UAE exit and UK entry

key_warnings: Tapeworm treatment for dogs has strict 24-120 hour timing window
Summer heat embargo (Jun-Sep) may prevent cargo transport from UAE
GB pet passport not valid for EU onward travel if needed
Breed-restricted dogs (Pit Bull type, Tosa, Dogo Argentino, Fila Brasileiro) CANNOT enter UK

tech_stack_recommendation.overview: Recommendation follows same architecture proven for the security-bodyguard project: Hugo static site generator + Cloudflare Pages hosting. The pet transport site has similar characteristics: data-driven page generation from JSON, high page count at scale, global audience, SEO-first requirements.

reasons: Scale: 5,000-10,000 pages at full build. Hugo generates this in under 10 seconds. Astro and Next.js struggle at this volume.
Data-driven: Hugo's data templates consume JSON directly. Our entire pipeline (Geographer -> Librarian -> Builder) outputs JSON. Perfect fit.
No JavaScript runtime: Pet transport pages are informational. No client-side interactivity needed for content. Pure static HTML = fastest load, best Core Web Vitals.
Proven: Same stack as security-bodyguard project. Shared learnings, shared templates where applicable.
Template system: Multiple page types (route, country, airline, breed, blog) map to Hugo's template hierarchy cleanly.
Incremental builds: Hugo supports partial rebuilds, needed when updating individual route pages without regenerating all 5,000+.

alternatives_considered.astro: Good for content sites with islands of interactivity. Node-based build slower at 5,000+ pages. Would pick if we needed interactive route comparison tools.
alternatives_considered.nextjs_ssg: Overkill. We don't need React. Build times 10-50x slower. Introduces unnecessary complexity.
alternatives_considered.11ty: Close second. More flexible templating than Hugo. Falls behind at 5,000+ pages. JavaScript ecosystem means slower builds.
alternatives_considered.custom_python: Considered a Python template engine (Jinja2) since research scripts are Python. Rejected: no sitemap/RSS generation, no dev server, would need to build tooling that Hugo provides for free.

reasons: Global edge: Pet transport customers are worldwide. Cloudflare has edge nodes in Dubai, Singapore, Hong Kong, Johannesburg, Sydney, London, etc.
Free tier: Unlimited sites, unlimited bandwidth, 500 builds/month. Generous for initial launch.
Cloudflare ecosystem: If we need WAF, DDoS protection, or rate limiting for the quote form, it's in the same dashboard.
File limit: 25,000 files max. Even at 10,000 pages with assets, we fit comfortably.
Build integration: Hugo is a supported framework. Connect GitHub repo and auto-deploy on push.

alternatives.netlify: Better developer experience (deploy previews, split testing). 100GB/month bandwidth cap. Weaker global edge in Asia/Africa/Middle East.
alternatives.aws_s3_cloudfront: Maximum control, more operational overhead. Consider if Cloudflare limits become a problem.

reasons: Zero third-party dependency. Form data stays within Cloudflare ecosystem.
Serverless function processes form submission, validates inputs, sends email to configured address.
Can add Turnstile (Cloudflare's CAPTCHA alternative) for bot protection. Free.
No data stored long-term. Form data forwarded to email and discarded.
quote_form.conversion_tracking: Google Tag Manager event on form submission. Track origin/destination pair for demand analysis.

domain.notes: Domain selection affects anchor text strategy and brand positioning. Options discussed in strategic questions (Task 0.10).

analytics.recommendation: Cloudflare Web Analytics + Google Search Console
reasons: Cloudflare Web Analytics is privacy-focused, free, no cookies. Built into Cloudflare Pages.
Google Search Console is essential for monitoring organic search performance, index coverage, Core Web Vitals.
Can add GA4 later if needed for detailed user behaviour analysis, but start lightweight.

build_pipeline.description: Source data -> Librarian assembly -> Hugo build -> Cloudflare deploy
steps: 1. Geographer outputs country, airline, breed data as JSON files (Blocks 3-7, DONE)
2. Librarian assembles route data by joining origin + destination + airlines + breeds (Phase 1)
3. Wordsmith writes route content, Interrogator writes FAQs, Optimiser writes SEO metadata (Phase 1)
5. Librarian exports final page data JSON files (one per page)
6. Builder runs Hugo with page data, selects template per page type, renders static HTML
7. Hugo generates sitemap.xml (split by content type if >50,000 URLs)
8. Deploy to Cloudflare Pages (git push triggers build, or manual deploy via Wrangler CLI)

data/.routes/: JSON files per route (assembled by Librarian)

layouts/.partials/: Shared components (header, footer, quote-form, faq-accordion, route-card)

static/.images/: Optimised images (country flags, hero images, icons)

site_root/.assets/: Source SCSS/CSS, unprocessed images (Hugo Pipes processes these)
